paper.title: Quantum information processing by nuclear magnetic resonance on quadrupolar nuclei
paper.venue: Philosophical Transactions of the Royal Society A

implemented_equations.quadrupolar_logical_decomposition: 3 Iz^2 - I(I+1) I = 3 ZZ for I=3/2
implemented_equations.grover_search: rho_f = (D O_m HxH) rho_00 (D O_m HxH)^dagger
implemented_equations.qst: signals from seven tomography rotations, then A vec(rho)=b

assumptions: This reproduction targets the encoded-logical-processor structure of the review, not a digitized experimental figure.
The computational basis follows the descending spin-projection order already used by the Na-23 code.
The identity component of a pseudo-pure state is kept in density matrices but interpreted as NMR-silent.
The algorithm benchmark is the minimal two-qubit Grover case because one Grover iteration is exact for N=4.
Tomography noise is synthetic and relative to the simulated complex tomography amplitudes.
